vulnerability.reason: Generated describes surgeons' reliance on coherent rationale without verification, which captures the same sensitivity as accepting anatomically plausible AI localization.
vulnerability.reference: Surgeons under time pressure accept AI localization if justification sounds anatomically plausible.
vulnerability.generated: Surgeons' reliance on coherent rationale narratives, without real-time cross-verification of spatial grounding, allows inconsistencies with image evidence to go undetected.

impact.reason: Injury to healthy tissue matches wrong-site surgery, incomplete pathology removal matches incomplete resection; patient harm is implicit.
impact.reference: Wrong-site surgery or incomplete resection from mislocalized target structures.
impact.generated: Surgical plans targeting wrongly localized structures cause injury to healthy tissue, incomplete pathology removal, or intraoperative conversion.

dose_response.reason: Both describe models producing inconsistent justifications leading to undetected reliance, wrong targets, and surgical harm.
dose_response.reference: Joint segmentation and rationale generation models produce visually grounded justifications inconsistent with actual evidence, enabling incorrect anatomical localizations that mislead surgeons in surgical planning, resulting in wrong-site surgery and patient harm.
dose_response.generated: Through surgeons’ use of interactive visual grounding tools, joint segmentation-rationale models produce visually grounded justifications inconsistent with image evidence, triggering undetected reliance on coherent, spatially-unverified narratives, leading to wrongly localized surgical targets causing tissue injury, incomplete removal, or conversion.
